title: Chapter/package project structure and whole-project verify
short_description: Support projects as packages with chapters as modules, documented, with whole-project verify running cross-scene continuity rules over 100+ beats in at most 30 seconds.
long_description_md:
# Chapter/package project structure and whole-project verify

## Why task exists

Phase 1-5 project shape is one `project.py` returning a flat list of Scenes. Fine for a 60-90s fixture or 2-minute mini-episode. Wrong shape for 15-20 min / 100+ beat episode: one file gets unmanageable, agent context window churns on a giant file for a one-scene edit, and cross-scene continuity/taste rules (`CONTINUITY.*`, `PACING.*` from `taste-constraint-rules`, plus `OVERLAP.*`/`MARGIN.*`/etc from `verify-layout-rules`) never got proven to scale past small fixtures. ROADMAP Phase 6 bullet 1, verbatim: "Chapter/package project structure supported and documented; whole-project verify runs cross-scene rules over 100+ beats in Ōēż30s." This task is that bullet, nothing else in Phase 6.

## Roadmap fit

First task in Phase 6 ("Long-form scale & performance") because everything else in the phase (parallel render, NFR targets, pipeline proof) needs a real multi-file long-form project to exercise against ŌĆö this task defines what that project looks like on disk and proves verify still scales once it's structured that way, not just when it's one giant flat list.

Depends on:
- **`taste-constraint-rules`** ŌĆö supplies `PACING.*`/`CONTINUITY.*` rule implementations this task's whole-project verify run must execute across chapter boundaries.
- **`verify-layout-rules`** ŌĆö supplies `OVERLAP.*`/`MARGIN.*`/`FONT.MIN_SIZE`/`TIMING.READ_TIME`/`CONTRAST.LOW` rules that also must run project-wide, not per-scene, once chapters exist.

## Required contract

**Package layout.** A project directory becomes an importable Python package: `project/__init__.py` (or equivalent root module) plus a `chapters/` directory where each chapter is its own module (e.g. `chapters/ch01_intro.py`, `chapters/ch02_invasion.py`). Each chapter module exposes an ordered list of `Scene` objects (or a typed `Chapter` wrapper with `id`, `title`, `scenes`) using the exact same scene-graph/timeline-compiler APIs single-file projects already use ŌĆö chapters are a project-structure and build-orchestration concern, never a new node/scene type. The root module declares chapter order (explicit list of chapter ids/modules, not filesystem-alphabetical, so reordering a chapter is an explicit code diff an agent can review).

**Build.** Extend the existing project-build path (graph construction ŌåÆ timeline compilation ŌåÆ immutable built artifact) to walk chapters in declared order, concatenating each chapter's scenes into one project-level Scene sequence with continuous beat/scene numbering and continuous map-state carry-over (map camera/fills/labels persist chapter-to-chapter exactly as they already persist scene-to-scene ŌĆö chapters introduce no new discontinuity). `REF.*`/structural errors must report which chapter+scene+beat they came from, not just a project-global index, so an agent editing one chapter file gets a file-scoped fix location.

**CLI.** `anima new --chapters` (or equivalent flag/subcommand) scaffolds a package-shaped project. `anima render`, `anima verify`, `anima preview`, `anima inspect` all accept a package-shaped `--project` path transparently ŌĆö no separate command family for chapter projects. A `--scene`/`--chapter` selector (reusing the existing partial-render mechanism) lets an agent target one chapter without rebuilding the whole project.

**Whole-project verify performance.** `anima verify` on a synthetic 100+ beat, multi-chapter fixture project must complete in Ōēż30s wall time on the reference machine (same bar Phase 2 already hit for a single 100-beat/1000-node project ŌĆö this task's job is proving the bar still holds once cross-scene continuity/taste rules from `taste-constraint-rules` and `verify-layout-rules` run project-wide across chapter boundaries, and once the project itself is package-shaped instead of one file). Reuses the existing spatial index; this task does not build new indexing, it validates the existing one holds at chapter scale and fixes any regression it finds.

**Documentation.** Chapter/package project structure is documented (docs site or in-repo cookbook-adjacent doc) with a worked example: what goes in `chapters/`, how ordering is declared, how map-state and continuity carry across chapter boundaries, how partial builds/renders target one chapter.

## Explicitly out of scope

- Implementing any actual continuity/taste/layout verification rule ŌĆö those are `taste-constraint-rules` and `verify-layout-rules`, consumed here, not built here.
- Per-scene parallel rendering ŌĆö `parallel-scene-render`.
- Hitting the full-episode NFR render-time/memory/fps bars ŌĆö `performance-nfr-targets`.
- Producing the actual 15-20 minute flagship-scale episode content ŌĆö `longform-pipeline-proof`; this task only proves the structure and verify-at-scale on a synthetic fixture.
- New spatial-indexing algorithm work ŌĆö reuses what Phase 2 shipped; only wiring/regression-fixing in scope.

deliverables.description: Package/chapter project structure implemented: chapters/ directory of modules each exposing an ordered Scene list, root module declares explicit chapter order, project build walks chapters in order producing one continuous built artifact with continuous beat/scene numbering and chapter-to-chapter map-state/continuity carry-over identical to existing scene-to-scene carry-over. REF.*/structural errors report chapter-scoped location.

CLI support: anima new --chapters scaffolds a package-shaped project; anima render/verify/preview/inspect accept a package-shaped --project transparently; --scene/--chapter selector targets one chapter without rebuilding the whole project.

Synthetic 100+ beat, multi-chapter fixture project verifies in <=30s wall time on the reference machine, exercising taste-constraint-rules and verify-layout-rules project-wide across chapter boundaries with zero unexpected findings drift versus a flat single-file equivalent.

Chapter/package project structure documented with a worked example (chapters/ layout, ordering declaration, continuity carry-over, partial-chapter build/render) checked into the docs source, covering every required topic.
